response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack/web developer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang background, but the role demands native-level Japanese, deep C/UNIX embedded firmware experience, Linux kernel modification, and LAN-switch security domain expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, switch chip SDKs) — none of which appear in the resume. The only C/C++ exposure is a brief stint at National Instruments a decade ago, and there is no Japanese language proficiency listed.
strengths: Strong general software engineering and testing/TDD discipline
Some past C/C++ experience at National Instruments on a developer tooling product
Broad multi-language exposure including Linux-based development
concerns: No native or any Japanese language proficiency listed (hard requirement)
No embedded firmware, LAN-switch, or network security domain experience (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, SNMP)
No evidence of Linux kernel source modification or recent production C/UNIX work

key_factors.factor: Minimal and dated C programming experience
